cards.title: Should Kids Climb Over Neighbour's Fence?
cards.body: A parenting couple debates whether it's acceptable for children to cross property boundaries to retrieve a ball. One parent worries it teaches trespassing; the other sees harmless fun.

cards.title: Dutch Walking Ritual Makes Kids Happier
cards.body: Dutch children participate in annual walking festivals lasting four nights, exploring neighbourhoods and building friendships. This tradition appears genuinely transformative for their wellbeing and health.

cards.title: Only Children Aren't Lonely Weirdos
cards.body: Parents with only children face persistent prejudices about raising selfish or maladjusted kids. New research suggests this stereotype is outdated and unfounded.

cards.title: Teaching Kids Right From Wrong
cards.body: Ethical choice-making doesn't require religious affiliation and doesn't have to be difficult. Parents can teach behaviour standards through accessible, practical methods.

cards.title: Neurodiversity Pride Matters for Kids
cards.body: Neurodivergent children benefit from understanding their identity as strength. Celebrating neurodiversity directly supports survival and wellbeing for these young people.

cards.title: How Stress Affects Children's Perception
cards.body: Even mild anxiety and stress distort how children perceive reality and process information. Emotional state directly impacts their ability to focus and learn.

cards.title: Father-Daughter Films Comfort Grieving Children
cards.body: Movies with strong father-daughter themes help children process grief and loss. These films provide comfort, especially for fatherless daughters on Father's Day.

cards.title: Sibling Relationships Drift in Adulthood
cards.body: Many siblings grow apart as they enter adult life for common, understandable reasons. Building stronger sibling bonds requires intentional effort and understanding.

cards.title: Teen Sensitivity to Graphic Content Normal
cards.body: Maturity has nothing to do with consuming content you find disturbing. Teens can develop healthy critical thinking by understanding their emotional responses.

cards.title: Cervical Cancer Deaths Reach Zero in Young Women
cards.body: School-age girls vaccinated against HPV since 2008 show zero cervical cancer deaths. Hundreds of lives have been saved through this preventive health programme.

cards.title: Social Media Ban Changing Children's Lives
cards.body: A proposed ban on social media for under-16s addresses widespread concerns about child wellbeing. Education officials discuss the potential benefits and implementation challenges.
